items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Identity` **Service directory:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` **Triggered by:** commit 8de13d337e88c073004f739210ed3073cc095c61 (PR #58097) by `@azure-sdk` (on behalf of `@micnash`) ## What Changed Version 1.21.0 of `Azure.Identity` was released alongside a version bump to 1.22.0-beta.1. The 1.21.0 release contains a significant architectural change: **all `Azure.Identity` public types have been moved into `Azure.Core`** and are exposed through `TypeForwardedTo` attributes. The package is now a lightweight facade. A new `MigrationGuide.md` was added to help users who may encounter `CS0433` compiler errors, but this guide is not linked or mentioned anywhere in `README.md`. ## Gaps Found - The `README.md` has no mention of the 1.21.0 type consolidation into `Azure.Core` — a major architectural change that users will encounter when upgrading - `MigrationGuide.md` exists at `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md` but is never referenced from `README.md` - The `Key concepts` section does not note that since `Azure.Core` 1.53+, types such as `DefaultAzureCredential` are available directly from `Azure.Core` without an `Azure.Identity` reference - The `Troubleshooting` section does not mention `CS0433` errors that can arise when mixing old `Azure.Identity` versions with new `Azure.Core`
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md` — **Edit** 1. **In the `Key concepts` section** (after the `Credentials` subsection paragraph, around line 35), add a new subsection about the type consolidation: ````markdown ### Type consolidation with Azure.Core Starting with version 1.21.0, `Azure.Identity` is a lightweight facade. All public types (including `DefaultAzureCredential`, `ManagedIdentityCredential`, and all other credential classes) are now compiled into `Azure.Core` and re-exported from `Azure.Identity` using [`TypeForwardedTo`]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) attributes. **This is a non-breaking change for most projects** — existing code continues to compile and run without modification. If you encounter `CS0433` compiler errors after upgrading (which can occur when an older `Azure.Identity` version is also present transitively), see the [Migration Guide][migration_guide]. ```` 2. **In the `Next steps` section** (around line 311), add a new subsection: ````markdown ### Migrating from older versions If you upgraded `Azure.Core` to 1.53.0 or later and are seeing `CS0433` compiler errors, see the [Migration Guide][migration_guide] for step-by-step remediation instructions. ```` 3. **In the `` section** at the bottom of `README.md`, add: ````markdown [migration_guide]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md ```` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new code snippets are needed for this documentation change — the content is explanatory prose and NuGet diffs already present in `MigrationGuide.md`. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections. Two sections need additions: - **Key concepts** — add the `Type consolidation with Azure.Core` subsection after the `Credentials` subsection - **Next steps** — add the `Migrating from older versions` subsection linking to `MigrationGuide.md` Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next. The snippet and API export scripts modify files in-place — those changes must be included in the commit. 1. `dotnet build sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 identity` — confirms no README snippet placeholders were accidentally broken. Check the git diff after running to confirm README was updated correctly 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 identity` — regenerates public API listing files under `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/api/`. No public API changes are expected, but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` — ensures consistent code formatting
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [Identity] Docs: README missing type consolidation notice and MigrationGuide link (v1.21.0)
